description: Web app for managing multiple pi-coding-agent sessions - Rust backend + Makepad WASM frontend with mobile support

userStories.title: Setup Rust backend project structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to set up the Rust backend foundation with Axum/Tokio web server.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize Cargo project with dependencies: axum, tokio, serde, serde_json, uuid, jsonrpc
Create basic folder structure: src/, config, templates
Create main.rs with Axum HTTP server listening on configurable port
Add CORS support for local development
Server starts successfully with 'cargo run'
Typecheck passes: cargo check
userStories.notes:
Codebase Patterns Established:

**Axum Server Setup:**
- Use tower-http::cors::CorsLayer with Any origin/methods/headers for development CORS
- Pattern: app.layer(CorsLayer::new().allow_origin(Any).allow_methods(Any).allow_headers(Any))
- Tokio async main with TcpListener for Axum serve
- Configure port via std::env::var("PORT").and_then(|p| p.parse()).ok().unwrap_or(DEFAULT)

**Project Structure:**
- src/main.rs - entry point with async main
- config/ - configuration directory (created, not yet used)
- templates/ - templates directory (created, not yet used)

**Dependencies:**
- axum = "0.8" for web framework
- tokio = { version = "1.41", features = ["full"] } for async runtime
- serde/serde_json for JSON serialization
- uuid for UUID generation
- tower-http for CORS middleware
- jsonrpc-core/jsonrpc-http-server for JSON-RPC support

**Health Check Pattern:**
- Simple GET /health endpoint returning status, service name, version
- Useful for server startup verification

**Gotchas:**
- Edition "2024" in Cargo.toml (newer than standard 2021)
- Ports 3000-3001 commonly in use, use uncommon ports for testing (e.g., 8765)
- Use timeout command to test server startup without blocking

userStories.title: Create project configuration structure
userStories.description: As a user, I need to configure which project root folders to scan for sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ProjectConfig struct with project_root_paths: Vec<PathBuf>
Create config file: config.toml in app root directory
Implement config loading from file with default empty list
Add config validation (paths must exist)
Create CLI args to override config file path
Typecheck passes: cargo check
userStories.notes:
Configuration System Implementation (US-002):

**thiserror Dependency:**
- The `#[source]` and `#[error]` attributes require `thiserror = "2.0"` crate
- Thiserror provides clean error enum derivation with Display and Error impls
- Pattern: #[error("Failed to {action} {path}: {source}")]

**Config Module Pattern:**
- ProjectConfig struct with Vec<PathBuf> for multiple paths
- from_file() returns default if file doesn't exist (graceful degradation)
- validate() checks path existence before runtime
- to_file() for config persistence (added but unused, ready for future)

**Priority Order for Overrides:**
- CLI args > Environment variables > Default values
- Example: port flag > PORT env var > 3000 default
- clap Parser with Option<T> for optional overrides

**Config File Template:**
- Create config.toml with documentation comments as examples
- Empty array [] for paths prevents errors on first run
- Users can uncomment and modify examples

**Main.rs Integration:**
- Load and validate config at startup
- Log configuration state (count of paths, warnings if empty)
- Parse CLI args before loading config for path override

**Acceptance Criteria Met:**
- ✓ ProjectConfig struct with project_root_paths: Vec<PathBuf>
- ✓ config.toml in app root with examples
- ✓ Config loading from file with default empty list
- ✓ Config validation (paths must exist)
- ✓ CLI args to override config file path (--config)
- ✓ Typecheck passes (cargo check)

userStories.title: Implement pi session discovery
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to discover pi sessions in each configured project folder.
acceptanceCriteria: Scan .pi/agent/sessions/ subdirectory in each project root
Parse session.jsonl files to extract session metadata (id, name, timestamp)
Create SessionInfo struct with: id, project_path, name, created_at, is_active
Implement discovery function: Vec<SessionInfo> scan_sessions(config)
Handle missing .pi directories gracefully
Typecheck passes: cargo check
userStories.notes:
Session Discovery Implementation (US-003):

**Session Discovery Pattern:**
- Scan .pi/agent/sessions/ subdirectory in each configured project root
- Parse session.jsonl files (JSONL format - one JSON object per line)
- Return Vec<SessionInfo> with all discovered sessions

**SessionInfo Struct:**
- id: String (session ID from sessionId field)
- project_path: PathBuf (path to the project containing the session)
- name: String (human-readable name, falls back to ID if empty)
- created_at: String (timestamp from JSON, defaults to "Unknown" if empty)
- is_active: bool (currently hardcoded to false, will be enhanced later)

**Graceful Degradation:**
- Missing .pi directories: Return empty Vec instead of error
- Missing session.jsonl: Return empty Vec instead of error
- Malformed JSONL lines: Skip and continue parsing (don't fail entire scan)
- Empty lines in JSONL: Skip gracefully

**JSONL Parsing Pattern:**
- Use BufReader with BufRead for line-by-line reading
- serde_json::from_str for each line
- SessionEntry struct with #[serde(rename)] for field name mapping
- Trim and skip empty lines before parsing

**Error Handling:**
- SessionError enum with IoError and ParseError variants
- Use thiserror for clean error derive macros
- Errors propagated via Result<> for file operations
- Public API (scan_sessions) never fails - returns empty Vec on errors

**Module Organization:**
- sessions.rs for session discovery logic
- SessionEntry private struct for internal parsing
- SessionInfo public struct for API responses

userStories.title: Create WebSocket server for real-time events
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a WebSocket server to stream pi events to the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: Add WebSocket support to Axum server (tokio-tungstenite)
Create /ws endpoint that accepts WebSocket connections
Create event channel for broadcasting to multiple clients
Define WSEvent enum: SessionStarted, SessionStopped, ThinkingDelta, MessageAdded
Implement connection management (track active clients)
Typecheck passes: cargo check
userStories.notes:
**WebSocket Implementation (US-004):**

**Axum WebSocket Integration:**
- Axum's WebSocket support uses tokio-tungstenite under the hood
- WebSocketUpgrade extractor upgrades HTTP to WebSocket connection
- Message::Text expects Utf8Bytes, not String - use .into() for automatic conversion
- split() separates WebSocket into Sender and Receiver for concurrent operations

**Broadcast Channel Pattern:**
- tokio::sync::broadcast::channel for pub/sub to multiple clients
- tx.subscribe() creates a new receiver that gets all future messages
- Channel capacity (100) provides backpressure if clients are slow
- Use _ to ignore send errors when no clients are connected

**Connection Management:**
- UUID for unique client identification
- Arc<RwLock<HashSet<String>>> for thread-safe active client tracking
- Clone values before moving into async closures if needed later
- tokio::select! waits for first completed task (cleanup on disconnect)

**State Management:**
- WSState must be cloned and passed via Router::with_state
- Clone is cheap due to Arc wrapping of internal state
- State accessible in handlers via State<T> extractor

**Gotchas:**
- Ping/Pong handling with split socket is complex - simplified for now
- Unused functions generate warnings but are expected (API for future stories)
- Edition "2024" works fine with axum 0.8 WebSocket features

userStories.title: Implement pi subprocess spawning with JSON-RPC
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to spawn pi processes in JSON-RPC mode and capture their output.
acceptanceCriteria: Create PiProcess struct managing subprocess: process, id, project_path, tx/rx channels
Spawn pi with: npx @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent --mode rpc --cwd <project>
Capture stdout (JSON-RPC events) and stderr (logs)
Parse JSON-RPC events: method, params, result
Create process manager: HashMap<SessionId, PiProcess>, max 10 concurrent
Kill process on request
Typecheck passes: cargo check
userStories.notes:
**Subprocess Spawning Pattern (US-005):**
- Use tokio::process::Command with .args() for spawning external processes
- .stdout(Stdio::piped()) and .stderr(Stdio::piped()) to capture output
- .spawn() returns Child with stdout/stderr handles that must be .take()

**Async Task Management:**
- tokio::spawn for concurrent stdout/stderr reading
- Store JoinHandle for task cleanup (abort on kill)
- Use underscore prefix (_stdout_task) to suppress unused variable warnings

**Broadcast Channel Pattern:**
- tokio::sync::broadcast::channel for pub/sub to multiple listeners
- tx.subscribe() creates new receivers for each listener
- tx.send() errors can be ignored when no listeners (use let _)
- Channel capacity (100) provides backpressure

**Process Management:**
- HashMap<String, PiProcess> for tracking by ID
- Check process.is_running() via process.id().is_some()
- Enforce concurrent limit (MAX_CONCURRENT_PROCESSES = 10)
- Remove from map before kill to prevent double-kill

**JSON-RPC Event Parsing:**
- JsonRpcEvent struct with Option<> fields for method/params/result
- Use #[serde(flatten)] with extra HashMap for flexible JSON
- Skip non-JSON lines gracefully (pi may output other content)
- Trim and skip empty lines before parsing

**Error Handling:**
- thiserror for clean error derive with #[source] attribute
- Contextual errors (include path/id in error variants)
- Return Err early for validation (path existence, UTF-8 check)

**Gotchas:**
- Unused variables in async closures: prefix with underscore
- PathBuf.to_str() returns Option<&str> - must handle None case
- Clone channels before moving into async closures
- Process.kill() is async - must await

userStories.title: Create HTTP API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need REST endpoints for listing projects and sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/projects - returns list of configured projects
GET /api/projects/:id/sessions - returns sessions for project
GET /api/sessions - returns all sessions across projects
GET /api/sessions/:id - returns session details and metadata
Add JSON response structs with proper serialization
Typecheck passes: cargo check
userStories.notes:
HTTP API Endpoints Implementation (US-006):

**Axum Router State Management Pattern:**
- When merging routers with .merge(), they must have the same state type
- Router<ApiState> cannot be merged with Router<AppState> - state types must match
- Solution: Keep ApiState as public struct in api.rs, have AppState wrap it
- Access nested state via State<AppState> then dereference (state.api_state.config)

**API Module Architecture:**
- ApiState wraps shared resources with Arc<RwLock<ProjectConfig>> for thread-safe mutable access
- Response structs (ProjectResponse, SessionResponse) need Serialize/Deserialize derives
- ErrorResponse implements IntoResponse for custom HTTP status codes (NOT_FOUND, BAD_REQUEST, etc.)
- Use Result<Json<T>, ErrorResponse> for handlers that can fail

**State Nesting Pattern:**
- AppState combines multiple module-specific states (ws_state, api_state)
- Individual modules (api, websocket) have their own state types
- AppState wraps ApiState rather than duplicating config access logic
- Handlers extract State<AppState> and access nested state (state.api_state.config.read().await)

**REST API Design Patterns:**
- GET /api/projects - list all projects with session counts
- GET /api/projects/:id/sessions - nested routes for hierarchical resources
- GET /api/sessions - list all sessions across all projects  
- GET /api/sessions/:id - individual resource access by ID
- Hash-based project IDs from paths for stable identification

**Move vs Clone Gotchas:**
- Strings don't implement Copy trait
- When moving String into struct field but also need it later as lookup key, use .clone()
- Pattern: let id = generate_id(); Struct { id: id.clone(), ... use id for lookup ... }

**Acceptance Criteria Met:**
- ✓ GET /api/projects - returns list of configured projects
- ✓ GET /api/projects/:id/sessions - returns sessions for project
- ✓ GET /api/sessions - returns all sessions across projects
- ✓ GET /api/sessions/:id - returns session details and metadata
- ✓ JSON response structs with proper serialization (ProjectResponse, SessionResponse, ErrorResponse)
- ✓ Typecheck passes (cargo check)

userStories.title: Setup Makepad WASM frontend project
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to setup the Makepad frontend project structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize Makepad project with cargo makepad-app --init
Configure Makepad for WASM compilation target
Create basic folder structure: src/, assets/, styles/
Setup HTTP fetch for calling backend API (localhost:PORT)
App loads in browser with blank screen (no build errors)

userStories.title: Create basic UI layout (sidebar + main panel)
userStories.description: As a user, I need a responsive layout with sidebar for navigation and main panel for content.
acceptanceCriteria: Create main UI with split layout: sidebar (30%) + main panel (70%)
Sidebar: collapsible with hamburger menu on mobile
Main panel: scrollable content area
Apply dark theme (dark gray background, light text)
Layout responsive to window resize
Typecheck passes: cargo check

userStories.title: Implement session list component
userStories.description: As a user, I need to see a list of sessions organized by project in the sidebar.
acceptanceCriteria: Create SessionList component fetching from /api/sessions
Group sessions by project (collapsible project headers)
Display session name and timestamp
Add visual indicator for running vs inactive sessions
Add search box to filter sessions by name

userStories.title: Implement session history view (static)
userStories.description: As a user, I need to view the message history of a selected session.
acceptanceCriteria: Create SessionHistory component displaying messages in chat bubble format
Fetch messages from backend endpoint /api/sessions/:id/messages
Render user messages (right-aligned) and assistant messages (left-aligned)
Display code blocks in monospace font with syntax highlighting placeholder
Support scrolling through message history

userStories.title: Connect WebSocket for live event streaming
userStories.description: As a user, I need to see live updates when sessions are active.
acceptanceCriteria: Connect to WebSocket at ws://localhost/ws on app load
Parse WSEvent messages: ThinkingDelta, MessageAdded, SessionStarted, SessionStopped
Update session list indicators in real-time (running/inactive)
Append new messages to SessionHistory as they arrive
Handle WebSocket reconnection on disconnect

userStories.title: Display thinking blocks in real-time
userStories.description: As a user, I need to see the assistant's thinking process as it happens.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ThinkingBlock component for rendering thinking sections
Subscribe to ThinkingDelta events via WebSocket
Append delta text to thinking block in real-time
Show 'Thinking...' spinner while waiting for first delta
Collapse/expand thinking block toggle

userStories.title: Implement chat input and message sending

Send messages via POST /api/sessions/:id/prompt endpoint

userStories.title: Implement session resume/spawn on selection
userStories.description: As a user, I need clicking a session to load it, spawning the pi process if not running.
acceptanceCriteria: On session click in sidebar, set as active session
Check if session process is running via backend state
If not running, call POST /api/sessions/:id/start to spawn in RPC mode
Load session history immediately (no waiting for process start)
Show loading state in UI while process is spawning

userStories.title: Make UI mobile-responsive

acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar collapses to full-width drawer on mobile (< 768px width)
